creator: kristinacors
caption:
Most people come here, waste hours deciding where to go, end up in average spots, and leave thinking NYC is overrated.

But there’s another version of this citythat feels electric, cinematic, and full of magic, you just need to know exactly where to go.

That’s why I created my 7 day NYC itinerary.

Every day is fully mapped out:
• The most aesthetic aesthetic cafés, rooftops & hidden gems

• Organized by neighborhood so everything flows effortlessly

• Exact travel times + opening hours included and logistics handled

No overthinking. No wasted time. No decision fatigue.

Just show up and experience New York the right way.

It’s only $29 right now and it will completely change your experience here.

Comment “New York City” and I’ll send it to you! #travelbucketlist #nycitinerary #thingstodoinnyc #nycluxury #nychiddengems

transcription.text: three places in New York City that will make you feel alive again. This first spot is a rooftop sitting on top of one of New York City's most luxurious hotels, the Ritz-Carlton. It has insane 360 views of the New York City skyline, both indoors and outdoors. And the drinks are top-notch. It's called Nubelus. This second spot is a rooftop located on the Lower East Side. And it's amazing during the day, but it's incredible on a summer night. It's called The Roof at Public Hotel. The third spot is one of my favorite for dancing, but before I share it, here's a mistake most people make in New York. They don't have a plan, so they waste time, end up in average spots, and miss the places that actually make the city feel alive. That's exactly why I created a seven-day New York City itinerary. And it's not just a list of spots. Every day is fully planned for you, from the most picturesque spots and rooftops to hidden gems. And it's organized by neighborhood, so your entire day flows effortlessly. Literally, every detail is handled, like travel time between stops for walking and driving and exact closing hours. So there's zero planning, zero friction, and zero decision fatigue. So come to New York City to shop my New York City itinerary. And by the way, the name of this third spot is Jimmy at the James Hotel. Follow for more.
